# case189_public_function_private_parameter_type — Public function newly gains a private parameter type

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Verdict** | 🟡 **COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK** |
| **Category** | Risk |
| **Platforms** | Linux |
| **Flags** | Bad practice |
| **Detected `ChangeKind`s** | `public_api_internal_dependency_added` |
| **Source files** | `examples/case189_public_function_private_parameter_type/` |

**Verdict:** 🟡 COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK · **Finding:** `public_api_internal_dependency_added` · **Evidence tier:** L5

> These cases ship a hand-built pair of evidence-model fixtures (`old.json` +
> `new.json`) instead of compiled `v1`/`v2` binaries, so the corpus is validated
> compiler-free by `tests/test_l3l4l5_examples.py`. See
> `scripts/gen_l3l4l5_examples.py`.

## What it demonstrates

In the derived L5 source graph the public function `demo::configure()` newly
carries a `DECL_HAS_TYPE` edge to the internal (non-public-header) type
`detail::Options` that it did not reach in v1 — a public function taking (or
returning) an internal type in its signature. Even though the *symbol* is new
territory (this is signature-visible, unlike case187/188's field/base
shapes), the risk is the same: an internal type's evolution is now something
callers of a public function are implicitly exposed to.

Sibling of `case187`
(`TYPE_HAS_FIELD_TYPE`) and `case188`
(`TYPE_INHERITS`) — together the three structural (non-body) kinds
`type_graph.py` populates from a Clang AST pass (ADR-041 P0 slice 1).

## Why no single artifact layer sees it

| Source | What it sees alone |
|--------|--------------------|
| Binary (L0/L1) | the exported symbol's mangled name does encode the parameter type, so a real ABI-level rename would show up as `func_added`/`func_removed` at L0 — but this fixture models the graph-level dependency fact directly, independent of whether the binary tier also proves a break |
| Header AST (L2) | `demo::configure`'s declaration is visible, but nothing marks `detail::Options` as *internal* relative to the public surface without cross-referencing header provenance |
| **Source graph (L5)** | the derived type-dependency delta (`DECL_HAS_TYPE`) → the finding, with explicit internal/public classification |

## How to fix

Either promote `detail::Options` to a documented, stable part of the public
API, or take/return only public types (e.g. a builder pattern, or an opaque
public handle that wraps the internal type).

---

- `new.json`
